text:
A UK firm has found a unique way to attract people back to offices after years of working from home - by installing the Olympic sport of bouldering.

Spacemade designs and creates flexible offices across the country, operates a 27,000 square foot hub in Birmingham, and aims to get the nation to return to our offices again.

And alongside the usual office furniture at 10 Brindley Place, it has installed a bouldering wall - to help persuade people to ditch WFM post-Covid.

The sport – a form of free climbing with crash mats - features in the Paris Games for the first time this year and Jonny Rosenblatt, who co-founded Spacemade in December 2019, says it’s all part of building a positive working environment.

Don't miss:Olympics LIVE: Lamecha Girma health update after athlete rushed to hospital

Don't miss:Simone Biles doubles down on MyKala Skinner comments after Olympics controversy

He explained: "We launched the company three months before the world was told never go to the office again!

"As we’ve come out of Covid you have to find a reason to get people back there and it’s got to be more than just because they’re contractually obliged to be. Employers have to work harder to get their staff to come in.

"Things like bouldering encourage competitive socialising and physical activity and the endorphin rush that goes with it. It sits very nicely alongside a positive working environment.

"The wall emboldens the team, gives them a reason to get together and enhances that time.

"Where it’s been used best is where companies take a couple of hours out of the day for a session and then maybe go for lunch.

"Not everyone is in the office every day like they used to be, but you want to make sure that those days when everyone is in are as meaningful and successful as they can be."

The move comes as new figures, suggest more than half the UK’s white-collar workers are still spending much of the week at home.

Data from CBRE, the property agent and building manager, suggests employers are looking for smaller but better office space.

Rosenblatt said: "There’s a lot of positivity about people being in the same place at the same time.

"The days of going to the pub at lunchtime have gone – the new generation of workers doesn’t have that same drinking culture. It’s at loggerheads with the idea of good health, wellness and fitness.

"But you still need social interactions, something in common that people are doing. That’s where we have tried to reposition where the office can be.

"It used to be the tea trolley and we shouldn’t call an end to that just yet!

"But the shift towards wellness activity goes hand in hand with office work – it’s a really nice marriage of two positive activities."

Spacemade, which has hubs in Leeds and London as well as the Midlands, offers flexible hot-desking for individuals as well as bigger spaces.

Its offices have a range of facilities including cafes, fully equipped gyms, spinning bikes, Pilates classes and podcast studios. Other ideas in the pipeline include a photography studio and a screening room.



Rosenblatt added: "These all elevate the space beyond a desk and a chair.

"We think about these things, so our members don’t need to think about them for their employees. They outsource it to us as a provider.

"We create unique spaces and individual brands for each building rather than having a cookie-cutter approach. Each space is pretty different.

"We want to provide something that is more than just an office. It’s a destination.

"Our ethos as a business is to build meaningful connections. Offering things like bouldering walls, peloton studios and gyms help enable that vision."

An instructor goes into Brindley Place on a weekly or bi-weekly basis to initiate new members on bouldering, which doesn’t need ropes or harnesses.

Rosenblatt has already been up the wall himself …but insists he’s not particularly good at it, remarking: "I enjoy it and it’s great fun though. It’s a bit of a leveller too – anyone can give it a go."

title: 2024 Olympics hack helps UK firm crackdown on work from home | UK | News | Express.co.uk
author: Jan Disley
description: EXCLUSIVE - British workers are being lured back to the office with a brand new Olympic sport.... bouldering.
